source: user accepted feedback in chat on 2026-05-14

exit_criteria: Accepted decision table covers navigation, layout, terminology, preferences, graph/map default scope, diagrams, and secondary-screen constraints.
Downstream documentation and implementation must start from build artifact rather than chat-only memory.

loop_start.reason: Feedback loop originated in current user conversation.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Accepted feedback should be validated before documentation/compiler work consumes it.

next_loop.reason: Documentation and implementation should consume this accepted feedback build, not prior chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

requirements.text: Simplify user-facing UI language and avoid backend jargon where possible.
Move top-level navigation into the header and remove left rail, right inspector/sidebar, and bottom status line.
Remove repeated section titles and one-line explanatory subtitles from default section chrome; use breadcrumbs/context only when helpful.
Rename Product/System top-level navigation into a single KB section with Product and System drilldown.
Within Product KB, expose Stories and UI surfaces as fast selectable lists with ASCII-like detail cards; users/personas remain contextual evidence.
Within System KB, use ASCII-like diagrams/cards and fix stacked/overlapping diagram rendering; component interactions should open detail cards.
Board should remain a Trello-like Kanban board while visually coherent with the ASCII-like design.
Rename Graph to Map and default it to documentation/KB relationship visibility; broader graph data should be secondary.
Rename Claims to Sessions in the user UI and focus on active sessions rather than claim internals.
Settings should represent repo-backed user preferences, not a visible path map to .codewiki/config.json; source paths may be advanced detail only.
Header should remove local-first wording and show only the local URL at top right with the settings icon.
The UI is a secondary-screen observability surface used alongside agent chat, not a terminal replacement.

summary: Accepted UI redesign direction for simpler ASCII-like CodeWiki web UI with header navigation, KB consolidation, Map naming, Sessions wording, repo-backed preferences, no sidebars, and no status line.

diff_table.current_state: Left rail, right inspector, and bottom status line frame most views.
diff_table.desired_state: Header navigation only; no persistent sidebars or status line; selected details appear inline as cards.
diff_table.rationale: Secondary-screen observability should not compete with agent chat or become a terminal replacement.
diff_table.risk: Inline detail flow must remain discoverable without sidebars.

diff_table.current_state: Top navigation exposes Product, System, Board, Graph, and backend-flavored wording.
diff_table.desired_state: Top navigation becomes Status, KB, Board, Map, Sessions.
diff_table.rationale: KB consolidates product and system knowledge; Map and Sessions are more user-friendly terms.
diff_table.risk: Backend graph/claims concepts need clean mapping to user labels.

diff_table.current_state: Status includes health, roadmap, graph, claims, gates, drift and broad metrics.
diff_table.desired_state: Status focuses on detailed state; health/drift are consequences, while roadmap/graph/session/gate concepts move to appropriate sections.
diff_table.rationale: Reduce duplication and make status a current-state detail page.
diff_table.risk: Must still expose blockers/gates where useful.

diff_table.current_state: Settings page flattens and displays .codewiki/config.json paths and source path prominently.
diff_table.desired_state: Settings acts as repo-backed preferences UI; source file/path is advanced detail only.
diff_table.rationale: Config exists so users can set preferences, not inspect file layout.
diff_table.risk: Editable settings need validation/policy; MVP may remain read-oriented but user-facing.

diff_table.current_state: Graph defaults to a broad generated graph slice with many artifact types.
diff_table.desired_state: Map defaults to documentation/KB relationships; other graph slices are secondary controls.
diff_table.rationale: Users mainly need to see how documentation relates without overload.
diff_table.risk: Filtering generated graph into doc-first map needs deterministic rules.

diff_table.current_state: System diagram rendering overlaps arrows/subtext/components and uses SVG graph-like boxes.
diff_table.desired_state: System KB uses ASCII-like diagrams/cards with cleaner routing and component detail selection.
diff_table.rationale: Terminal-like design should improve readability and match new UI style.
diff_table.risk: Complex diagrams may require iterative layout refinement.

accepted_decisions.summary: Use repo-backed preferences as durable settings truth; browser state only for temporary view state like selected tab, collapse, zoom, or scroll.
Do not introduce React for the first implementation; use current browser-native HTML/CSS/JS server unless future complexity justifies a framework.
Use ASCII-like terminal visual style in the web UI without turning the agent terminal/chat into the UI.
Keep source paths and backend records available only under advanced/source disclosure.

assumptions: 'nui' in user feedback means UI surfaces.
The standalone web UI lives in extensions/codewiki/src/adapters/web/control-room.ts.
open_questions: Which repo config keys should become editable preferences first?
Should the first Map doc relationship view derive only from KB docs or also include tasks/build evidence that link docs?
non_goals: No direct canonical graph edits from UI.

knowledge: Update .codewiki/kb/product/uis/control-room.md and related UI docs to Status, KB, Board, Map, Sessions organization and ASCII-like design.
Update system UI docs if needed to describe renderer behavior and source-backed preferences.
roadmap: Create or update implementation task for ASCII-like web UI redesign.
code: Refactor standalone Control Room web UI navigation/layout/rendering in extensions/codewiki/src/adapters/web/control-room.ts.
